title: buildah run --net=none bind mounts /etc/hosts
body:
<!--
If you are reporting a new issue, make sure that we do not have any duplicates
already open. You can ensure this by searching the issue list for this
repository. If there is a duplicate, please close your issue and add a comment
to the existing issue instead.

If you suspect your issue is a bug, please edit your issue description to
include the BUG REPORT INFORMATION shown below. If you fail to provide this
information within 7 days, we cannot debug your issue and will close it. We
will, however, reopen it if you later provide the information.

---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST
-->

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->
I need to add static host map entries to /etc/hosts during build. But buildah mounts generated file even if network is set to none. Changes to this file also don't persist.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. run buildah with --net=none
2. make changes /etc/hosts and exit
3. mount the container, /etc/hosts file is empty


**Describe the results you received:**
```
buildah run --isolation=chroot --net=none debian-working-container /bin/bash
root@ff257a6158f3:/# cat /etc/hosts
# Generated by Buildah
127.0.0.1   localhost localhost.localdomain localhost4 localhost4.localdomain4
::1         localhost localhost.localdomain localhost6 localhost6.localdomain6
127.0.1.1	fedora # prltoolsd vm name
127.0.0.1   ff257a6158f3
::1         ff257a6158f3
root@ff257a6158f3:/# echo "128.0.0.4 fdev" > /etc/hosts
root@ff257a6158f3:/# cat /etc/hosts
128.0.0.4 fdev
root@ff257a6158f3:/# exit
[root@fedora ~]# m=$(buildah mount debian-working-container)
[root@fedora ~]# cat $m/etc/hosts
[root@fedora ~]#
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
/etc/hosts should not be bind mounted if network is none
Changes to /etc/hosts should persist.

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
buildah-1.15.0-1.fc32.x86_64
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:         1.15.0
Go Version:      go1.14.3
Image Spec:      1.0.1-dev
Runtime Spec:    1.0.2-dev
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:
image Version:   5.5.1
Git Commit:
Built:           Wed Dec 31 19:00:00 1969
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman version` if reporting a `podman build` issue:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

```

```

```

```

**Output of `cat ~/.config/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
[storage]
driver = "vfs"
rootless_storage_path = "$HOME/.local/share/containers/storage"
[storage.options]
[storage.options.vfs]
ignore_chown_errors = "true"
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
[storage]
driver = "vfs"
runroot = "/var/run/containers/storage"
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage
rootless_storage_path = "$HOME/.local/share/containers/storage"
[storage.options]
[storage.options.vfs]
ignore_chown_errors = "true"
```

comments.body: This is fixed in upstream, Closing.
